_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Research communications trends in emerging technologies (in computer and telephony networks, satellite, cable, and wireless) in both open and classified sources.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences, including paper writing, conference presentation, and internal briefing formats.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-K-013 (TF, cosine: 0.309), AI-K-020 (AI, cosine: 0.305).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.7/10 (raw 0.465). Shared vocabulary: [research]. DCWF-only terms [cable, classified, communications, computer, emerging, networks] vs WIDAI-only terms [audiences, briefing, communicating, conference, findings, formats] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.851 (P=0.847, R=0.854). Balanced P/R — the 21-word DCWF element and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (82.7%). "Research communications trends in emerging technologies (in computer and telepho" and "Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical aud" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.335 < STS 0.465 (gap -0.130). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [cable, classified, communications] and [audiences, briefing, communicating] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.5%. Shared tokens: [research]. DCWF-only: [cable, classified, communications, computer, emerging, networks]. WIDAI-only: [audiences, briefing, communicating, conference, findings, formats].
